thesis: A reasonably-valued state energy major where analysts are upgrading hard; classic value-with-a-catalyst that pairs cheapness with improving estimates.
falsification: Crude/realisation weakness drives revisions back down, or the stock lags despite the upgrade cycle over the next quarter.

thesis: Embedded-value-cheap life insurer where analyst estimates are being revised up; a structural re-rating candidate as VNB margins and persistency surprise.
falsification: Revisions roll over or new-business margins disappoint, removing the catalyst behind the cheapness.

thesis: City-gas distributor where margins and volumes are getting upgraded; cheap relative to its own history with an improving estimate trend.
falsification: APM gas allocation cuts or spot-LNG spikes compress margins and revisions turn down.

thesis: Refining-margin cyclical trading cheap with analysts upgrading; a high-beta value catalyst play sized modestly for its volatility.
falsification: GRMs compress and the revision tailwind reverses, or the stock proves a pure value trap.

thesis: Gold-financier benefiting from elevated gold prices and AUM growth, with analysts revising up; a defensible mid-cap value-growth blend.
falsification: Gold price reversal or regulatory tightening on LTV cuts AUM and revisions fade.

thesis: Low-multiple agri-input name where subsidy clarity and volume strength are lifting estimates; a cheap cyclical with a catalyst.
falsification: Subsidy/policy shock or weak monsoon pushes estimates down and the value stays trapped.

thesis: Low-cost iron-ore miner trading cheap with improving estimates on volume and pricing; keep as a core cyclical value position.
falsification: Iron-ore prices fall hard or volume guidance is cut, dragging revisions and the stock.

thesis: Holdco/cement-and-VSF conglomerate at a holdco discount with improving estimates; structural value as paints/B2B scale.
falsification: Paints-foray losses widen or cement pricing weakens, reversing the revision uptrend.

thesis: Mid-cap IT compounder with order-book momentum and analysts upgrading; adds quality-growth at a reasonable price to a value book.
falsification: Deal momentum slows or BFSI/discretionary IT spend cuts revisions and de-rates the multiple.

thesis: Low price-to-book regional bank where asset quality and ROA are improving and estimates rising; deep-value bank with a turn.
falsification: Slippages spike or NIM compresses, reversing the earnings upgrade thesis.

thesis: SFB trading below book with improving credit cost and growth; analysts upgrading into a recovery.
falsification: MFI/unsecured stress re-accelerates and revisions roll back over.

thesis: Integrated low-cost aluminium/alumina producer leveraged to firm LME and alumina prices, with analysts revising up; a cheap commodity cyclical.
falsification: LME aluminium / alumina prices fall and revisions reverse.

thesis: Diversified shipping/offshore play trading at low multiples with firm tanker rates and improving estimates; a cash-rich value cyclical.
falsification: Freight rates collapse or offshore day-rates soften, pulling revisions down.

thesis: Cheap, high-yield coal monopoly with improving volume and e-auction realisations; a defensive value cyclical with a fundamental catalyst.
falsification: Volume miss or sharp e-auction premium decline drags earnings and revisions.

thesis: Standalone health insurer riding retail-health premium growth with analysts upgrading; a structural compounder at a reasonable entry.
falsification: Loss ratios deteriorate or growth slows, reversing upward estimate revisions.

thesis: Well-capitalised NBFC scaling new secured products with improving estimates; a growth-at-reasonable-price financial.
falsification: Asset-quality slippage in new books or NIM pressure cuts revisions.

thesis: Reasonably-valued large private bank with steady ROA and improving estimates; an anchor financial for a value book.
falsification: Credit cost normalises higher or deposit competition compresses NIM and revisions fade.

thesis: Best-in-class private bank compounding book value with stable revisions; the quality anchor of the financials sleeve.
falsification: NIM compression or a credit-quality scare reverses the steady upgrade trend.

thesis: Largest PSU bank at low price-to-book with healthy ROA; cheap enough to hold even as revisions cool, on valuation support.
falsification: Asset-quality deterioration or a capital raise dilutes book and the cheapness proves a trap.

thesis: High-yield diversified resources holdco at a holdco discount, leveraged to commodities and the demerger unlock; positive momentum and value.
falsification: Commodity downturn or balance-sheet/debt stress at the parent reverses the thesis.

thesis: Aluminium demerger entity held for the value-unlock from the Vedanta split; modest weight given no analyst coverage.
falsification: Demerger stalls or aluminium economics deteriorate, removing the unlock case.

thesis: Iron-and-steel demerger entity held for the split-driven re-rating; sized modestly pending coverage.
falsification: Demerger delay or steel-spread compression undermines the unlock value.

thesis: Power demerger entity held for the Vedanta value-unlock; modest weight without coverage.
falsification: Demerger stalls or power-tariff/PLF economics weaken.

thesis: Oil-and-gas demerger entity held for the split re-rating; small structural position.
falsification: Demerger delay or upstream economics deteriorate.

thesis: Line-pipe maker with a healthy order book; trim slightly as revisions cool but keep for the cyclical value case.
falsification: Order inflows slow or margins compress and the stock de-rates.

thesis: Integrated steel producer at low EV/tonne with a volume-expansion story; a cheap cyclical value hold.
falsification: Steel spreads collapse or the expansion capex overruns, hurting returns.

thesis: Regulated-return power utility with renewables optionality; trim slightly on softer revisions, keep for defensive value.
falsification: Capex execution slips or the renewables ramp disappoints, capping the re-rating.

thesis: Cash-generative IT major at a reasonable multiple with stabilising estimates; quality ballast for the book.
falsification: Discretionary IT spend weakens further and revisions turn negative.

thesis: Large IT services name held at a trimmed weight pending a clearer revision turn; valuation reasonable.
falsification: Margin pressure or weak deal bookings push revisions down.

thesis: Turnaround IT name kept at a reduced weight while its margin-recovery and revision trend are still soft.
falsification: Margin recovery stalls and revisions keep falling.

thesis: BFSI-heavy mid IT with decent estimates; trim modestly as the revision tailwind eases.
falsification: BFSI tech-spend cuts drag deal momentum and revisions lower.

thesis: Housing-finance turnaround below book with improving asset quality and growth; a value financial with a re-rating path.
falsification: Growth disappoints or credit costs rise, capping the re-rating.

thesis: High-ROA affordable-housing financier with a long growth runway; a structural compounder at a fair price.
falsification: Asset-quality stress in affordable housing or NIM compression cuts ROA.

thesis: Discount-broker leveraged to retail activity; trim as revisions soften with cooling market volumes, keep for optionality.
falsification: Retail trading volumes fall sharply or regulation hits the take-rate.

thesis: Commodity-exchange near-monopoly with operating leverage as volumes grow; a structural moat at a fair multiple.
falsification: Volume growth stalls or tech/cost issues recur, denting the operating leverage.

thesis: Specialty-chemicals maker seeing an earnings recovery as the chemical cycle turns, with analysts upgrading; a cyclical value-growth add.
falsification: Chemical pricing stays weak or new-capacity returns disappoint, reversing revisions.

thesis: Niche high-margin exporter gaining share with improving estimates; a quality structural compounder at a reasonable price.
falsification: Mining-capex slowdown or pricing pressure cuts volumes and revisions.

thesis: Largest NBFC-MFI recovering from a credit-cost peak with analysts upgrading; a deep-value financial leveraged to normalisation.
falsification: Microfinance asset quality re-deteriorates and the revision recovery stalls.

thesis: Sterile-injectables exporter with a margin and revenue recovery underway and analysts upgrading; a value-growth pharma add.
falsification: US injectables pricing/regulatory issues or China-parent overhang reverse the recovery.

thesis: API-and-formulations maker with margin recovery and capacity additions; a cyclical value pharma I keep.
falsification: API pricing weakness or regulatory action at a key plant hurts earnings.

thesis: US-generics turnaround with improving cash flows; keep at a slightly reduced weight as the revision trend moderates.
falsification: US generic pricing pressure resumes and the turnaround stalls.

thesis: Largest Indian pharma with a growing specialty franchise; a defensive quality anchor for the healthcare sleeve.
falsification: Specialty-pipeline setbacks or US-generic weakness reverse estimates.

thesis: Diversified pharma with a strong US pipeline and India branded growth, trading reasonably with analysts upgrading; a value-growth add.
falsification: Key US approvals slip or pricing erodes, reversing the revision uptrend.

thesis: Diversified EPC contractor at a low multiple with a strong order backlog converting to revenue; a cheap execution-leveraged hold.
falsification: Execution slippage or working-capital stress drags margins and the multiple.

thesis: Power-and-industrial EPC plus O&M with a large order book and improving execution; a cheap structural growth hold.
falsification: Order inflows slow or receivables stretch, hurting cash conversion.

thesis: Debt-free hydrocarbon-engineering consultancy with a recovering order book and analysts upgrading; a high-RoE value cyclical.
falsification: Order inflows from refining/petrochem capex slow, reversing revisions.

thesis: Defence-electronics PSU with multi-year order visibility and high RoE; keep as a structural growth hold despite a full valuation.
falsification: Order finalisations slow or margins normalise, de-rating the premium.

thesis: Powertrain and industrial machining supplier diversifying end-markets; a structural auto-component value-growth hold.
falsification: Auto-volume slowdown or integration issues from acquisitions hurt margins.

thesis: Diversified auto-component supplier gaining content-per-vehicle; a steady structural value hold.
falsification: OEM demand softens or input-cost pressure compresses margins.

thesis: Instrument-cluster and sensors supplier riding premiumisation and EV content; a structural value-growth hold.
falsification: 2W demand weakness or share loss reverses the content-growth story.

thesis: Tube and engineered-products platform with new-business optionality; a quality structural hold at a reasonable price.
falsification: New-venture losses widen or core auto demand slows, capping returns.

thesis: Integrated sugar-and-ethanol producer with distillery optionality; trim as revisions cool but keep for the ethanol-policy value case.
falsification: Adverse sugar/ethanol pricing or policy changes weaken margins.

thesis: Largest listed developer with a robust launch pipeline and net-cash balance sheet; a structural realty-upcycle hold.
falsification: Property demand cools or launches slip, denting pre-sales momentum.

thesis: South-and-NCR developer with improving pre-sales and deleveraging; a value play on the housing upcycle.
falsification: Sales velocity slows or debt rises, reversing the deleveraging thesis.

thesis: Realty name with a data-centre optionality story; trim modestly given soft price momentum and a full valuation while keeping the optionality.
falsification: Data-centre rollout slips or realty sales weaken, puncturing the premium.

thesis: Improving-ROA private bank below peer multiples with steady growth; a value bank held for the re-rating runway.
falsification: Asset-quality slippage or NIM compression reverses the ROA-improvement story.

thesis: Dominant private bank digesting its merger with normalising metrics, trading at a reasonable multiple; a core quality anchor.
falsification: Deposit-growth or NIM disappointment delays the re-rating.

thesis: Diversified retail-lending NBFC at a low multiple; trim as revisions soften, keep for the cheap-financial value case.
falsification: CV-cycle downturn or credit costs rise, hurting the growth-and-quality mix.

thesis: High-quality life insurer with strong distribution; trim on softening revisions while keeping a toehold.
falsification: Margin or persistency disappointment continues to pressure estimates.

thesis: Premium life insurer with soft near-term estimates; trim and hold a small position pending a revision turn.
falsification: VNB-margin pressure persists and revisions keep falling.

thesis: Leading hospital network with a digital-health optionality; trim on a full valuation while keeping the structural healthcare exposure.
falsification: Pharmacy/digital losses widen or hospital occupancy softens.

thesis: Niche electrical-laminations supplier riding electrification capex; a small-cap structural value hold.
falsification: Order momentum slows or input-cost pass-through fails, hurting margins.

thesis: Diversified industrial-materials compounder with steady RoCE; a quality structural hold.
falsification: Industrial demand softens or the EV/ceramics bets disappoint.

thesis: Dominant auto-and-architectural glass maker with content growth; a structural value hold despite soft near-term revisions.
falsification: Auto demand weakens or energy costs spike, compressing margins.

thesis: Large renewables developer with a big capacity pipeline; trim given a rich valuation and high leverage while keeping a small option on execution.
falsification: Funding costs rise or capacity-add execution slips, straining the balance sheet.

thesis: Infrastructure incubator with airports and new-energy optionality; trim to a small structural position given valuation and complexity.
falsification: Capex/funding strain or governance concerns re-rate the group down.

thesis: Asset-heavy less-than-truckload logistics player leveraged to formalisation and freight recovery; a small-cap value hold.
falsification: Freight demand stays weak or fuel costs squeeze margins.

thesis: Loyalty/CRM SaaS with global clients; keep a small position as an optionality bet pending a clearer revision trend.
falsification: Customer churn rises or growth decelerates, undermining the SaaS thesis.

thesis: Airport monopoly leveraged to traffic and tariff resets; trim on falling revisions while keeping a small traffic-recovery option.
falsification: Traffic growth disappoints or tariff orders go against the operator.
